threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still think it’s normal to Uber everywhere instead of hopping on the bus or bike. Local transport is a rhythm, not a luxury.
Last Friday, a bloke on the 59 showed off his tattoo of a fried egg while arguing with the driver about the best late-night takeaway in Brixton.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: I knew I was home when I walked past the Späti at 3 AM, and the owner was playing 90s techno while chatting with his regulars like it was a street party.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin can you sip your coffee at an old tram depot turned café while watching street art battles unfold on nearby walls.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: Locals know to avoid Civic Center during BART meltdowns. Tourists still standing there, confused, when the station's in smoke and chaos.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy juggling while waiting for the Muni, then he handed a dollar to the bus driver as the doors opened. Only in SF.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is all about busy squares and bars, but it’s the quiet streets where you hear the real life—kids playing and families chatting.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a newcomer by their confusion at the T-10 ticket machines—stickers still on the buttons and fumbling for change.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You call it a 'sandwich' and not a 'butty.' Also, you still think the buses are punctual.
Last night on the 196, a lad serenaded us with a ukulele cover of 'Wonderwall.' Must be the late-night Brixton spirit!

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: I realized I belonged when I saw the Turkish bakery open at 5 AM, the queue wrapped around the block, all chatting like it was just the morning greeting.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Friedrichshain can you find an eclectic mix of vegan döner stands next to traditional currywurst shops, all packed with locals at 3 AM.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: Locals don’t flinch at the sound of sirens; it’s the tourists jumping out of the way that give themselves away.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught the 47 at Civic Center, but it sat there for ages. Turns out a dog was chilling on the tracks, just enjoying the ride. Classic SF.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think all bars serve tapas, but in Gràcia, many focus on craft cocktails. The vibe shifts dramatically once you step off the main streets.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can always spot them by their five-gear bikes, struggling up the steep streets, while locals glide by on electric scooters.
